signals.title: Trump says Iran deal could be reached in 'two or three days' and Strait of Hormuz will reopen 'immediately'
signals.summary: Speaking to reporters after attending the NBA Finals in New York, Trump said the two parties are in the final stages of a "very, very good deal".

signals.title: Trump booed before Knicks lose to Spurs at Madison Square Garden in NBA Finals Game 3
signals.summary: Trump's decision to attend Game 3 of the NBA Final was controversial for Knicks fans, some of whom feared he would jinx what has been an epic playoff run.

signals.title: NASA to select new headquarters building by end of year
signals.summary:
NASA plans to find a new headquarters building by the end of this year while remaining in the Washington area. 
 The post  NASA to select new headquarters building by end of year  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declines Senate testimony on AI, China and exports
signals.summary: Sen. Elizabeth Warren said Nvidia’s CEO should answer questions publicly as lawmakers scrutinize AI chip sales to China and export controls.

signals.title: Corning strikes another multibillion-dollar AI deal. What the new Amazon pact means for the stock
signals.summary: Amazon joins the laundry list of partnerships Corning has secured in 2026, which includes Meta Platforms and Nvidia.

signals.title: Isar Aerospace raises 270 million euros for global launch expansion
signals.summary:
European launch company Isar Aerospace has raised 270 million euros ($312 million) to support its global expansion as it prepares for its next launch attempt. 
 The post  Isar Aerospace raises 270 million euros for global launch expansion  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Commercial launch providers shortlisted for China’s low-cost cargo spacecraft
signals.summary:
China is expanding the role of its commercial rocket companies by shortlisting four such entities for launches of a new space station cargo transport system. 
 The post  Commercial launch providers shortlisted for China’s low-cost cargo spacecraft  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Let’s procure in-orbit services like Earth observation data and services
signals.summary:
The Chinese company MizarVision recently gained attention with its open source Earth observation data analysis over the Middle East which showed images of F-35s  gathering 24 hours before Operation Epic […] 
 The post  Let’s procure in-orbit services like Earth observation data and services  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Judge blocks Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee
signals.summary: Trump had imposed the $100,000 fee in order to restrict the program, arguing that it has undermined U.S. security through the "replacement of American workers."

signals.title: Congress moves toward approving $70 billion for ICE and CBP through Trump's presidency
signals.summary: The U.S. House is expected to vote this week on a $70 billion measure to fund Immigration and Customs Enforcement and Customs and Border Protection after delay.

signals.title: 51% of U.S. adults say the American Dream is out of reach for most people right now: CNBC survey
signals.summary: In an exclusive CNBC survey, 81% of respondents said the cost of living is their biggest financial hurdle to achieving the American Dream.

signals.title: Nuvalent shares surge 39% after UK's GSK agrees to buy the cancer drugmaker for $10.6 billion
signals.summary: The deal comes at a time of biotech dealmaking frenzy, driven by looming patent cliffs, newly buoyant public markets and drugmakers' race to bolster pipelines.

signals.title: NASA interested in Hubble reboost if costs can be reduced
signals.summary:
As NASA prepares an attempt to reboost an astronomy spacecraft in a decaying orbit, it is open to doing something similar for Hubble, if its operating costs can be reduced. 
 The post  NASA interested in Hubble reboost if costs can be reduced  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: NASA concerns about Russian repairs prompted ISS safe haven decision
signals.summary:
NASA directed astronauts on the ISS to shelter in a Crew Dragon spacecraft after Russian cosmonauts planned to perform repairs the agency thought created “elevated risk” to the station. 
 The post  NASA concerns about Russian repairs prompted ISS safe haven decision  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: USDA Secretary Rollins calls Texas ag chief 'unserious' amid screwworm threat
signals.summary: Texas Agriculture Commissioner Sid Miller lost his bid for a fourth term in a March Republican primary, despite Trump's endorsement.

signals.title: India's growth story faces its toughest test yet in Modi’s third term
signals.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi faces his biggest test as India's appeal as the world's fastest-growing economy dims for global investors.

signals.title: The space race is coming for pharma: Why drug development is heading to lower Earth orbit
signals.summary: Companies see a commercial opportunity in creating new ways to administer drugs to patients – in space.

signals.title: China's May shipments to U.S. clock 5-year high growth at 35% as overall exports jump on tech boost
signals.summary: Exports to the U.S. surged the most since March 2021, extending a rebound following a long streak of double-digit declines for the most of last year.

signals.title: [13F] Berkshire Hathaway (Buffett) — nuovo filing 2009-11-05
signals.summary: Controlla il filing per vedere le nuove posizioni aperte/aumentate.

signals.title: Big, power-ready facilities drive industrial real estate market
signals.summary: Companies are looking for modern facilities that can accommodate power-hungry automation, industrial experts said in a report first provided to Facilities Dive.

signals.title: Can stadiums be energy efficient? USGBC map shows that many of them are
signals.summary: The U.S. Green Building Council has conferred LEED status on 31 stadiums in North America, from the 9,500-seat Southwest University Park in El Paso, Texas, to the 88,000-seat Estadio Banorte in Mexico City.

signals.title: FERC approves SPP non-firm, large-load transmission service
signals.summary: The Southwest Power Pool service aims to help data centers and other large loads get online quickly, but they can have their service cut when grid conditions are tight.

signals.title: Behind-the-meter data center gas plants will raise US energy bills
signals.summary: Counterintuitively, it is data centers’ independence from the grid and use of natural gas that will hike energy costs for homes and businesses, write experts from Energy Innovation.

signals.title: Convicted FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried files formal request for Trump pardon
signals.summary: The co-founder of the now defunct FTX crypto exchange is currently serving a 25-year federal prison sentence.

signals.title: Trump nominates Todd Blanche for attorney general amid controversy over DOJ fund
signals.summary: Blanche previously served as a criminal defense lawyer for President Trump.

signals.title: Perplexity plans IPO in 2028 regardless of what happens to Anthropic or OpenAI, CEO tells CNBC
signals.summary: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas' comments come after AI giant Anthropic confidentially filed to go public.

signals.title: Electric vehicle giant BYD predicts 80% of China car sales will soon be electric
signals.summary: BYD expects China's competitive electric vehicle market to continue growing, even as manufacturers and analysts anticipate domestic demand to taper further.

signals.title: Sam Bankman-Fried officially asks Trump for a presidential pardon
Rising load growth reshapes cooperative portfolios and strategy
signals.summary: Large loads pose additional challenges for cooperatives. Planning strategically is critical.

signals.title: The benefits of a unified billing, payment, communications platform
signals.summary: One platform. Fewer silos. Better billing and payment experiences for utilities and customers.

signals.title: How live conversations can close the gap between awareness and enrollment for load flexibility
signals.summary: The data on outbound calling: higher enrollments, strong sentiment, surprising insights

signals.title: FCC lets Amazon Leo miss deployment deadline with temporary spectrum penalty
signals.summary:
Amazon no longer faces a July 30 cutoff for deploying half its planned 3,232 broadband satellites, but the reprieve comes with a temporary loss of spectrum priority that could give SpaceX and other rivals more leverage in orbit. 
 The post  FCC lets Amazon Leo miss deployment deadline with temporary spectrum penalty  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Russia is jamming GPS from space
signals.summary:
“America is at risk of high impact GPS jamming and spoofing from space” was the title of my SpaceNews opinion article in October 2024. Little did I know that its […] 
 The post  Russia is jamming GPS from space   appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.summary:
For anyone who wasn’t sure whether China was in it to win the space race and dominate the rapidly growing space economy, its filings in December for 200,000 more satellites […]

signals.title: Speed as a Strategic Advantage in Border Monitoring
signals.summary:
Across Europe, border environments are becoming increasingly dynamic and complex. Activity can shift within hours—vehicles reposition, staging areas disperse, small watercraft alter routes, and nodes of activity appear and disappear […]

signals.title: US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet strikes, disables oil tanker in Gulf of Oman
signals.summary: An F/A-18 Super Hornet assigned to the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln fired a precision munition into the vessel's engineering and steering spaces.

signals.title: Germany and France drop joint fighter jet project
signals.summary: Failure to reach an agreement underscores the struggles Europe has faced in rebuilding its military capacity after decades of underinvestment.

signals.title: US must learn lessons from Ukraine, innovate faster and cheaper: Anduril president
signals.summary: Anduril President Christian Brose discussed the need to develop cheaper weapons systems at scale to avoid quick depletion of exquisite munitions.

signals.title: Polish-Ukrainian startup develops radar to track elusive, low-flying drones
signals.summary: The technology aims to find small, low-flying drones, including those connected to operators via long, fiber-optic command wires.

signals.title: French jet on NATO mission shoots down drone in Latvian airspace
signals.summary: Military drones straying into the airspace of Russia’s neighbors have stoked concerns that Russia’s war in Ukraine is spilling over NATO’s borders.

signals.title: French Army has recruitment surplus but lacks equipment, deputy chief says
signals.summary: “This year, we’re already slowing down recruitment because not only are we recruiting better, but we’re retaining people better," a service leader said.

signals.title: Asia chip-linked shares recover after U.S. peers bounce back
signals.summary: In the U.S., chip stocks powered gains on Monday, helping the S&P 500 gain 0.3%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite advanced 0.86%.

signals.title: Jim Cramer warns key pillars of the bull market are beginning to crumble
signals.summary: CNBC's Jim Cramer said that he's becoming more cautious on stocks after several pillars of his bullish outlook have come under pressure.

signals.title: Airlines find the grass isn't always greener with new engines
signals.summary: Airline CEOs complained that manufacturers aren't making enough of their engines and that they're falling short on reliability.

signals.title: OpenAI confidentially files for IPO, prepping Wall Street for mega AI debut
signals.summary: OpenAI's confidential filing lands days before SpaceX is set to go public and a week after Anthropic announced its confidential disclosure with the SEC.

signals.title: Retirement 'underspending' is risky, advisor says. Here's why
signals.summary: Retirees often focus on the risk of spending too much in retirement. But spending too little poses another kind of danger.

signals.title: Silicon Valley’s new buyout playbook is hitting Wall Street
signals.summary: Instead of selling AI tools to companies, venture firms are buying legacy companies outright and rebuilding them around AI from the inside.

signals.title: Chip rebound sparks hedging flurry from traders
signals.summary: Despite the overall bearish sentiment on the cohort, traders put a few bullish bets on potential semiconductor winners.

signals.title: Food supply 'not at risk' after new Texas screwworm cases, USDA secretary says
signals.summary: The U.S. will lean on the same playbook it did starting in the late 1950s, part of which involves releasing sterile insects to suppress the pest's population.

signals.title: Chip rebound has one trader buying protection
signals.summary: Chip stocks are bounding back Monday, but the rebound doesn't mean tech investors are in the clear, according to Mike Khouw.

signals.title: Household worries over finances hit highest level since July 2022, New York Fed survey shows
signals.summary: While the central bank's monthly survey showed the inflation outlook mostly unchanged, the general perception of conditions deteriorated.

signals.title: Weight loss drug maker sinks 23% after new safety data spooks investors
signals.summary: While the experimental drug met its key targets, analysts are concerned over the commercial opportunity due to side effects.

signals.title: Kalshi traders say Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal before January
signals.summary: Two weeks ago traders on Kalshi saw a 60% chance that the Strait of Hormuz would open before August. Now traders see a higher chance it won't open before 2027.

signals.title: UK startup NewOrbit raises $18.5 million in Series A round
signals.summary:
SAN FRANCISCO – NewOrbit Space, a UK startup developing satellites for very low Earth orbit (VLEO), has raised $18.5 million in a Series A investment round. With the funding, announced […] 
 The post  UK startup NewOrbit raises $18.5 million in Series A round  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: OQ Technology plans direct-to-smartphone demo with cellular spectrum
signals.summary:
Luxembourg’s OQ Technology plans to test direct-to-smartphone satellite connectivity next year in Germany using a local telco’s cellular spectrum, setting up a challenge to U.S.-led services in the emerging market. 
 The post  OQ Technology plans direct-to-smartphone demo with cellular spectrum  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: Rounding up the space unicorns
signals.summary:
Once rare beasts, billion-dollar startups are multiplying across new orbital markets. 
 The post  Rounding up the space unicorns  appeared first on  SpaceNews .

signals.title: US strikes Iranian sites after Iran launches drones, in latest Gulf flare-up
signals.summary: U.S. forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites on Saturday after shooting down drones launched by Iran toward the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. military said.
